Off Calendar Oct-10-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Robert Sellards, Esq., for defendant/cross-complainant and moving party: Nane Jemo

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant/Cross-Complainant Nane Jemo's Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition is granted. Notice Of Motin And Motion To Compel Responses From Deft Dean Alac To Pltf'S Supplemental Document Request;Supplemental Interrogatories;Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Oct-02-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
John H. Reilly, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Dean Alac; no other appearances.

TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff Adam M. Conway's Motion To Compel Responses From Defendant Dean Alac To Plaintiff's Supplemental Document Request, Supplemental Interrogatories, And Special Interrogatories is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant Dean Alac's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant Dean Alac is ordered to respond within 10 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $600, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Plaintiff Adam M. Conway'S Motion To Compel Responses From All Three Named Defendants To: 1- Document Request Set #1; 2-Form Interrogatories And 3-Request For Admissions #1
Off Calendar Jan-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
John H. O'Reilly, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Adam M. Conway

No other appearance stated. AFTER HEARING, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED.
1) plaintiff Adam Conway's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories as to Dean Alac, Dina Alac, and Nane Jemo
2) plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents as to Dean Alac, Dina Alac, and Nane Jemo.
The Court has read and considered the late-filed opposition. The motions are moot as supplemental responses have been served. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to the supplemental responses. The Court notes that the general statements and objections are not authorized by the Code of Civil Procedure and should be stricken.
Defendant shall produce verifications and documents by February 1, 2008. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.
